label: Cerumen impaction without infection or foreign body
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of cerumen impaction or earwax blockage.
No ear infection, foreign body, trauma, eardrum perforation, ear tube, acute hearing loss, or neurologic ear sign documented.
Medication or removal plan is clinician-entered.
exclusion_criteria: Otitis externa, otitis media, mastoiditis, malignant otitis externa risk, severe systemic illness, or immunocompromised pathway.
Foreign body, button battery, ear trauma, cotton swab injury, bleeding, perforated eardrum, ear tube, or specialist-directed ENT plan.
Sudden hearing loss, severe dizziness or vertigo, facial weakness, or other neurologic ear sign.
must_not_miss: Ear foreign body or button battery.
Neurologic cause of dizziness or facial weakness.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, or ENT as instructed if hearing or fullness does not improve.

source_audit.notes: AAO-HNS patient education supports earwax as usually protective, symptomatic impaction framing, clinician-directed removal options, and avoiding cotton swabs or ear candles. Autonomous Phase 1002-1500 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-confirmed cerumen impaction without infection, foreign body, perforation, tube, acute hearing loss, neurologic ear signs, or specialist-directed ENT plan.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. AAO-HNS patient education supports earwax as usually protective, symptomatic impaction framing, clinician-directed removal options, and avoiding cotton swabs or ear candles. Autonomous Phase 1002-1500 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-confirmed cerumen impaction without infection, foreign body, perforation, tube, acute hearing loss, neurologic ear signs, or specialist-directed ENT plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of cerumen impaction
no infection, foreign body, eardrum perforation, tube, acute hearing loss, or neurologic ear sign
